title: shorea falciferoides subsp. falciferoides
intro: Shorea falciferoides subsp. falciferoides, also known as the Kalampag tree, is a species of tree in the family Dipterocarpaceae. It is a medium-sized to large tree native to the Philippines and is valued for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Shorea falciferoides subsp. falciferoides include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
